children.heading: Filing and review of claims
children.label: a Filing and review of claims

children.text: the filing of a claim by the covered employee alleging the violation, as provided in ;section 1402 of this title
the preliminary review of the claim, to be conducted by a hearing officer as provided in ;section 1402a of this title
mediation as provided in , if requested and agreed to by the parties under that section; andsection 1403 of this title

a formal hearing as provided in , subject to Board review as provided in and judicial review in the United States Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit as provided in .section 1405 of this titlesection 1406 of this titlesection 1407 of this title

children.heading: Right of employee to file civil action
children.label: b Right of employee to file civil action

children.heading: Civil action
children.label: 1 Civil action
children.text: Only a covered employee who has filed a claim timely as provided in and who has not submitted a request for a hearing on the claim pursuant to may, during the period described in paragraph (3), file a civil action in a District Court of the United States with respect to the violation alleged in the claim, as provided in .section 1402 of this titlesection 1405(a) of this titlesection 1408 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the preliminary review of the claim by the hearing officer as provided in shall terminate upon the filing of the action by the covered employee; andsection 1402a of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the procedure for consideration of the alleged violation shall not include any further review of the claim by the hearing officer as provided in .section 1402a of this title

children.heading: Period for filing civil action
children.label: 3 Period for filing civil action
children.text: The period described in this paragraph with respect to a claim is the 70-day period which begins on the date the covered employee files the claim under .section 1402 of this title

children.heading: Special rule for employees who fail to state a claim for which relief may be granted
children.label: 4 Special rule for employees who fail to state a claim for which relief may be granted
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (3), if a covered employee receives a written notice from the hearing officer under that the employee has the right to file a civil action with respect to the claim in accordance with , the covered employee may file the civil action not later than 90 days after receiving such written notice.section 1402a(d)(2) of this titlesection 1408 of this title

children.heading: Special rule for Architect of the Capitol and Capitol Police
children.label: c Special rule for Architect of the Capitol and Capitol Police
children.text: In the case of an employee of the Office of the Architect of the Capitol or of the Capitol Police, the Office, after receiving a claim filed under , may recommend that the employee use the grievance procedures of the Architect of the Capitol or the Capitol Police for resolution of the employee’s grievance for a specific period of time. Any deadline in this chapter relating to a claim for which the employee is using the grievance procedures, that has not already passed by the first day of that specific period, shall be stayed during that specific period.section 1402 of this title

children.heading: Election of remedies for Library of Congress
children.label: d Election of remedies for Library of Congress

children.heading: Direct Act
children.label: A Direct Act
children.text: The term “direct Act” means an Act (other than this Act), or provision of the Revised Statutes, that is specified in section 1311, 1312, or 1313 of this title.

children.heading: Direct provision
children.label: B Direct provision
children.text: The term “direct provision” means a provision (including a definitional provision) of a direct Act that applies the rights or protections of a direct Act (including rights and protections relating to nonretaliation or noncoercion) to a Library claimant.

children.heading: Library claimant
children.label: C Library claimant
children.text: The term “Library claimant” means, with respect to a direct provision, an employee of the Library of Congress who is covered by that direct provision.

children.heading: Election after proceedings initially brought under this chapter
children.label: 2 Election after proceedings initially brought under this chapter
children.text: A Library claimant who initially files a claim for an alleged violation as provided in may, at any time before the date that is 10 days after a hearing officer submits the report on the preliminary review of the claim under , elect to bring the claim for a proceeding before the corresponding Federal agency under the corresponding direct provision, instead of continuing with the procedures applicable to the claim under this subchapter or filing a civil action in accordance with .section 1402 of this titlesection 1402a(c) of this titlesection 1408 of this title

children.heading: Election after proceedings initially brought under other civil rights or labor law
children.label: 3 Election after proceedings initially brought under other civil rights or labor law
A
children.text: continue with the agency’s procedures and preserve the option (if any) to bring any civil action relating to the claim, complaint, or charge, that is available to the Library claimant; or

children.label: B
children.text: file a claim with the Office under and continue with the corresponding procedures of this title available and applicable to a covered employee.section 1402 of this title

children.heading: Timing
children.label: 4 Timing
children.text: A Library claimant who meets the initial deadline under for filing a claim under this subchapter, or any initial deadline for bringing a claim, complaint, or charge under the applicable direct provision, and then elects to change to alternative procedures as described in paragraph (2) or (3)(B), shall be considered to meet any initial deadline for the alternative procedures.section 1402(d) of this title

children.heading: Application
children.label: 5 Application
children.text: This subsection shall take effect and shall apply as described in section 153(c) of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2018 () (except to the extent such section applies to any violation of or a provision of an Act specified in ).Public Law 115–141section 1331 of this titlesection 1331 of this title

children.heading: Rights of parties to retain private counsel
children.label: e Rights of parties to retain private counsel
children.text: Nothing in this chapter may be construed to limit the authority of any individual (including a covered employee, the head of an employing office, or an individual who is alleged to have committed personally an act which consists of a violation of part A of subchapter II) to retain counsel to protect the interests of the individual at any point during any of the procedures provided under this title for the consideration of an alleged violation of part A of subchapter II, including as provided under with respect to individuals subject to a reimbursement requirement of .section 1415(d)(8) of this titlesection 1415(d) of this title

children.heading: Standards for assertions made by parties
children.label: f Standards for assertions made by parties

children.text: No pleading, written motion, or other paper is presented for any improper purpose, such as to harass, cause unnecessary delay, or needlessly increase the cost of resolution of the matter.

The claims, defenses, and other legal contentions the party advocates are warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for extending, modifying, or reversing existing law or for establishing new law.
The factual contentions have evidentiary support or, if specifically so identified, will likely have evidentiary support after a reasonable opportunity for further review or discovery.
The denials of factual contentions are warranted on the evidence or, if specifically so identified, are reasonably based on belief or a lack of information.

children.heading: Procedure
children.label: g Procedure
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to supersede or limit .section 1361(d)(2) of this title
